sanskrit: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् । यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥
hindiTranslation: यत्न करनेवाले योगीलोग अपनेआपमें स्थित इस परमात्मतत्त्वका अनुभव करते हैं ।  परन्तु जिन्होंने अपना अन्तःकरण शुद्ध नहीं किया है, ऐसे अविवेकी मनुष्य यत्न करनेपर भी इस तत्त्वका अनुभव नहीं करते ॥
englishTranslation: The striving yogis see this supreme reality abiding in themselves. The unrefined and thoughtless, even while striving, do not see it.
essence: Seeing the supreme reality depends on inner purification, not effort alone.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People who have disciplined and purified themselves can realize the supreme reality within. Effort without inner refinement is not enough.
simpleInsight: Real progress feels less like forcing and more like becoming fit to see.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Krishna keeps answering Arjuna’s deeper confusion, not just his fear of fighting. After describing how the inner being moves through bodies and senses, Krishna says only the disciplined can perceive the supreme reality within themselves.
modernRelevance: You sit in silence after a fight, trying to understand why the same pattern keeps repeating. More information does not help until your inner state changes enough to see what was hidden.
translationLiteral: यतन्तः (striving) / योगिनः (yogis) / च (and) / एनम् (this) / पश्यन्ति (see) / आत्मनि (in the self) / अवस्थितम् (abiding) । / यतन्तः (striving) / अपि (even) / अकृतात्मानः (those without a purified inner being) / न (not) / एनम् (this) / पश्यन्ति (see) / अचेतसः (the thoughtless)
insight: It shifts the goal from trying harder to becoming inwardly clear enough to perceive what is already present.

quoteVariants.text: Purified striving reveals the supreme reality. Mere effort cannot.
Effort alone misses it; inner cleansing changes sight.
Clean the mind. Then see clearly.
